changes: Added a new validation path in src/publish/validator.ts
Hardened error handling around the GitHub API client

files.description: New validation path with retry and backoff.
Tests for the new validation path.

findings.body: The retry loop multiplies backoff by 2 on every iteration but never caps the maximum delay. A pathological 5xx storm could push the delay into hours; clamp to a sane ceiling.
findings.reasoning: Exponential backoff without a cap is a known footgun. The diff shows the multiplier and initial delay but no max-delay constant. Effect: a long-running outage stalls the publish step for hours instead of failing closed.
findings.title: Retry backoff lacks a maximum-delay cap

findings.body: When the API client raises `RequestError` with status 422, the retry path treats it as transient and retries forever (until the cap above). 422 is permanent — surface and abort.
findings.reasoning: Status 422 is GitHub's validation error; retrying does not help. The new code does not differentiate between 5xx and 4xx in the retry classifier.
findings.title: 422 misclassified as transient

summary: Incomplete review — one or more chunks did not produce output and were merged without their findings. The visible diff covers two correctness issues in retry/backoff handling, but the remainder of the patch was not analyzed by Codex on this run. Re-run after lowering max-chunk-bytes.
